environment: 2. Database: PostgreSQL users and auth_sessions tables via TypeORM repositories
3. API entry points: POST /auth/login, POST /auth/logout, and POST /auth/refresh
4. Evidence source: auth.service.ts plus the authored auth.service.spec.ts session-flow unit tests
project.notes: Feature-based workbook for FN-02 Authentication Session. A focused auth unit-test suite was authored for the session lifecycle, but this checkout does not include server/node_modules so Jest could not be executed. Cases remain Untested until dependencies are installed and the suite is run.

changeLog.description: Initial workbook generated for login, logout, refresh-token rotation, same-device replacement, and session-cap cleanup logic.

features.name: Authentication Session
features.testRequirement: Validate login authentication, session persistence, same-device revocation, logout behaviors, refresh-token rotation, and active-session cleanup for the implemented authentication session flow.

functions.name: AuthService.login
functions.description: Authenticate the user, issue access and refresh tokens, and persist a new auth session.
functions.preCondition: A user account exists and the submitted login payload satisfies DTO validation.

functions.name: AuthService.logout
functions.description: Revoke either one matching active session or every active session owned by the caller.
functions.preCondition: The caller has at least one active session.

functions.name: AuthService.refreshToken
functions.description: Rotate tokens for a valid unexpired active session and reject invalid or orphaned session states.
functions.preCondition: An active auth session exists with an unexpired refresh token hash.

functions.name: AuthService.createAuthSession
functions.description: Revoke same-device sessions, clean expired sessions, hash the refresh token, and save a new session row.
functions.preCondition: A login already authenticated the user successfully.

functions.name: AuthService.cleanupExpiredSessions
functions.description: Delete expired sessions and revoke older active sessions once the configured cap is exceeded.
functions.preCondition: The user has expired sessions or at least five active unexpired sessions.

testCases.description: Log in successfully with valid credentials and persist a new auth session.
testCases.procedure:
1. Submit valid email and password.
2. Provide userAgent and ipAddress metadata.
3. Observe the returned tokens and session save call.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. accessToken and refreshToken are returned.
2. A new auth session is saved with hashed refreshTokenHash, the supplied userAgent, the resolved ipAddress, isRevoked = false, and a future expiresAt value.
3. Existing same-device sessions are revoked before the new session is saved.
testCases.dependencies: Existing user account with a bcrypt-hashed password and working JWT signing.
testCases.note: Mapped to the authored service spec for the successful login branch.

testCases.description: Reject login when the submitted email does not match any user account.
testCases.procedure:
1. Submit an unknown email with any password.
2. Observe the authentication result.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. UnauthorizedException is thrown.
2. No session is saved.
testCases.dependencies: The submitted email is absent from the users table.
testCases.note: Covers the missing-user branch in AuthService.login.

testCases.description: Reject login when the password is incorrect for an existing user.
testCases.procedure:
1. Submit an existing email with a wrong password.
2. Observe the authentication result.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. UnauthorizedException is thrown after password comparison fails.
2. No session is saved.
testCases.dependencies: The email belongs to an existing user but the password does not match.
testCases.note: Covers the invalid-password branch in AuthService.login.

testCases.description: Log out only the session whose refresh token matches one active session.
testCases.procedure:
1. Seed multiple active sessions for the user.
2. Submit logout with a refresh token that matches exactly one session.
3. Inspect the revoke update.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. Only the matching session is updated with isRevoked = true and revokedAt set.
2. The logout success message is returned.
testCases.dependencies: At least one active session hash matches the supplied refresh token.
testCases.note: Covers the token-specific branch in AuthService.logout.

testCases.description: Log out all active sessions when no refresh token is supplied.
testCases.procedure:
1. Call logout without a refreshToken body field.
2. Inspect the repository update criteria.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. Every non-revoked session for the user is updated to isRevoked = true.
2. The logout success message is returned.
testCases.dependencies: The user owns one or more active sessions.
testCases.note: Covers the bulk-revocation branch in AuthService.logout.

testCases.description: Refresh tokens successfully when the supplied token matches an active unexpired session.
testCases.procedure:
1. Seed a non-revoked unexpired session.
2. Submit refresh with the matching token.
3. Inspect the response and update call.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. A new access token and refresh token are returned.
2. The matching session row is updated with a new hashed refreshTokenHash and lastUsedAt timestamp.
testCases.dependencies: An active session exists for an existing user and the supplied token matches its stored hash.
testCases.note: Covers the main token-rotation path in AuthService.refreshToken.

testCases.description: Reject refresh when no active unexpired session matches the submitted token.
testCases.procedure:
1. Submit an invalid token or use a token tied to a revoked or expired session.
2. Observe the refresh result.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. UnauthorizedException is thrown.
2. No session hash is rotated.
testCases.dependencies: The submitted token does not match any valid active session.
testCases.note: Covers the invalid-token branch in AuthService.refreshToken.

testCases.description: Reject refresh when the session exists but the owning user record no longer exists.
testCases.procedure:
1. Seed a valid session.
2. Make the user lookup return null.
3. Observe the refresh result.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. UnauthorizedException is thrown.
2. No new token pair is returned.
testCases.dependencies: The session still exists but its owning user cannot be loaded.
testCases.note: Covers the orphaned-session branch in AuthService.refreshToken.

testCases.description: Replace an older active session from the same device before persisting the new session.
testCases.procedure:
1. Call createAuthSession with a userAgent that already has an active session.
2. Inspect the revoke update and the saved replacement session.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. Existing same-device sessions are revoked.
2. The refresh token is hashed.
3. A replacement active session is saved with the supplied device metadata.
testCases.dependencies: A prior active session already uses the same userAgent string.
testCases.note: Mapped to the direct private-method unit test.

testCases.description: Delete expired sessions before the active-session cap is evaluated.
testCases.procedure:
1. Seed expired sessions for the user.
2. Call cleanupExpiredSessions.
3. Inspect the delete criteria.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. Sessions older than the current time are deleted first.
testCases.dependencies: The user has expired session rows.
testCases.note: Derived from the direct cleanup unit test.

testCases.description: Revoke older active sessions when five or more unexpired sessions already exist.
testCases.procedure:
1. Seed at least five active sessions.
2. Call cleanupExpiredSessions.
3. Inspect which sessions are revoked.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. Older sessions beyond the retained slice are updated with isRevoked = true and revokedAt set.
testCases.dependencies: The user already has five or more active unexpired sessions.
testCases.note: Boundary case for the active-session cap.
